# Database Metadata

This folder is intentionally lightweight compared to the equivalent folder
in `emerion-load-service`: **emerion-dashboard never queries Firebird
directly** — all legacy-schema knowledge (table/column names, Firebird data
types, NULL semantics) lives in `emerion-load-service`'s
`database-metadata/` folder instead.

What belongs here is the **PostgreSQL** schema this service owns —
generated/maintained via Flyway migrations in
`infrastructure/src/main/resources/db/migration/` (in the `:infrastructure`
Gradle module).

## Current Schema

### `customer` (V1__create_customer_table.sql)
| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `BIGINT GENERATED BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY` | surrogate PK |
| `external_id` | `VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL UNIQUE` | Firebird legacy PK, ingestion upsert key |
| `name` | `VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL` | |
| `email` | `VARCHAR(255)` | nullable |
| `status` | `VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'UNKNOWN'` | `ACTIVE`/`INACTIVE`/`UNKNOWN` |
| `created_at` | `TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()` | |
| `updated_at` | `TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()` | bumped on every ingestion update |

Indexes: `idx_customer_name` (`LOWER(name)`, for case-insensitive partial
search), `idx_customer_status`.

## Keeping This Up To Date
When a new Flyway migration is added, update this file with the new/changed
table shape — treat it as a human-readable mirror of
`infrastructure/src/main/resources/db/migration/`, not a replacement for
reading the actual SQL.

## Cross-Reference to emerion-load-service
If you need to know what a `customer.external_id` actually corresponds to
in the legacy Firebird schema (which table/column it was extracted from),
that mapping lives in `emerion-load-service`'s
`.github/database-metadata/firebird-schema-portal_2024_01.md` — not here.
